title: OrganiGram Holdings (OGI) Q1 2021 Earnings Call Summary
text: OrganiGram Holdings Q1 2021 Earnings Call Summary. Financial Results - Revenue Performance: Adult-use recreational net revenue grew 30% year-over-year to $16.8 million. Gross recreational revenue increased 42% to $22.5 million. Total net revenue declined to $19.3 million from $25.2 million due to lower wholesale sales. Key Metrics: Generated positive cash flow from operations ($0.3 million) in Q1. Ended quarter with $134 million in cash and short-term investments. Net loss of $34.3 million ($0.17 per share diluted basis). Adjusted EBITDA: negative $6.8 million. Operational Highlights - Product Portfolio Expansion: The company launched 53 new SKUs since July with 14 more expected before February. Notable releases include three high-THC Edison strains. Capacity Constraints: Management identified $5-6 million in unfulfilled purchase orders during Q1. The company plans to hire 100 cultivation and 30 packaging staff to address demand. Market Position: SHRED pre-shredded flower became the #1 most-searched brand on the Ontario Cannabis Store website for both November and December. Forward Outlook: CEO Greg Engel emphasized that the company remains focused on cost management and has identified meaningful upside potential for gross margins to drive profitability.
